body.title: Vetinari: gap map against autonomous directive vision
body.result: 7-dimension gap map: directive-intake/decompose/clarification/dispatch/tracking/signal/CP-audit all MISSING or PARTIAL. Filed 7 follow-on tickets (T-vetinari-directive-intake through T-vetinari-cp-audit). Design doc: D-vetinari-gap-map-2026-06-08.md
body.intention: I intend that we know exactly which capabilities Vetinari has, which are missing, and what tickets are needed to reach 'Akien gives a directive, Vetinari sorts it with team and reports when deployed, guided by the six core patterns.'
body.description:
Vetinari (devices/vetinari/device.py) exists with factory registry, health rollup, and Akien escalation. The target vision: Akien gives a natural-language directive ('make the web server do X'), Vetinari decomposes it into work, coordinates with Akien (clarifying questions), Librarian (research), CC (build), DickSimnel (token-light tasks), tracks progress, and reports when deployed — guided by CP1-CP6.

This is a gap-mapping sprint, not an implementation sprint. Read the existing device, evaluate each capability dimension against the vision, produce a gap list, and file one follow-on ticket per gap.

Capability dimensions to evaluate:
1. **Directive intake** — can Vetinari receive a natural-language goal from Akien via bus/channel?
2. **Decomposition** — can he break a directive into tickets or work units?
3. **Clarification loop** — can he ask Akien questions when a directive is ambiguous (CP1: 'I don't know' → ask)?
4. **Team dispatch** — can he route work to CC (build), Librarian (research), DickSimnel (token-light), Granny (coordination)?
5. **Progress tracking** — can he track whether work items are done and the directive is fulfilled?
6. **Deployment signal** — can he detect 'deployed' and report back to Akien?
7. **CP alignment** — does each behavior reflect the six core patterns? CP1 (admits uncertainty), CP3 (explains why each dispatch decision was made), CP4 (minimizes friction), CP6 (safety-conscious escalation)?

For each dimension: PRESENT (code exists, tested), PARTIAL (code exists but incomplete/untested), MISSING (not present).

**Affected files:** devices/vetinari/device.py, devices/vetinari/__init__.py, config/profiles/vetinari.yaml, tests/vetinari/ (read only — this is evaluation)
**Design rules:** none apply — evaluation sprint only; no code changes
**Scope boundary:** In scope: read existing Vetinari device + test suite, evaluate against 7 dimensions, produce gap list, file one ticket per gap. Out of scope: implementing any gap (those are the follow-on tickets this sprint produces).
**Completion criteria:** Gap map document written to ~/.unseen_university/vetinari/gap_map_20260607.md listing each dimension as PRESENT/PARTIAL/MISSING with one-sentence evidence. At least one follow-on ticket filed per MISSING or PARTIAL dimension. Sprint closes with a summary posted to shared channel so Akien sees the gap count.
